top_signals.title: Ziemba: Iran Looking to Monetize & Weaponize Hormuz
top_signals.summary: Oil steadied after falling more than 3% in previous sessions following early progress in peace talks over the Iran war. A US waiver allows some sales from Iran, offering them a crucial economic lifeline. Rachel Ziemba, Founder of Ziemba Insights spoke to Bloomberg’s Abeer Abu Omar on Horizons Middle East and Africa on the energy markets response to the US-Iran talks. (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Singtel sells $773 million Thai energy stake as it ramps up AI and data center investments
top_signals.summary: Southeast Asia's largest telecom operator sold 2.8% stake for about S$1 billion in Gulf Development.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Hormuz Traffic Picks Up as More Tankers Cross With Open Signals
top_signals.summary: More ships are openly signaling their intention to traverse the Strait of Hormuz, pointing to growing confidence among shipowners and traders about sending vessels through the chokepoint as tensions ease.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Nomura Joins Sony in Powering Big Week for Japan Bond Sales
top_signals.summary: Nomura Holdings Inc. and Sony Group Corp. are leading a slew of deals from Asia in global bond markets on Tuesday, in what is shaping up to be one of the biggest weeks this year for Japanese issuance overseas.

top_signals.title: Morgan Stanley Weighs Expansion to Dallas With $1.33 Billion Office-Building Deal - WSJ
top_signals.summary: Morgan Stanley Weighs Expansion to Dallas With $1.33 Billion Office-Building Deal  WSJ
